titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 1797
GA 1797
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Κεφαλαιον Α removed from incipit by David Parker.
πατρος moved from 6.39 to 6.38 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 20.4 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 21.6 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 16.21 corrected, superline in 1.45 removed by David Parker.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number: 1797Name of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 133rBase text used: TRns Transcribed by: ACMTranscription begun: 19.12.12Transcription finished: 18.1.13 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - Yes Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: 12.45 whole verse omitted

ab.note: There are traces of another shape as well as the o, treated here as hesitation in scribendo
The scribe seems to have had difficulty in deciding on the correct strokes in writing the ending of this word
The first hand reading had a smooth breathing after the epsilon and possibly an accent on the final syllable. The fourth letter may have been μ or ν.
The scribe may have partly written η before writing ει.
The scribe seems to have written at least one other stroke before settling on the letters αι
The scribe wrote at least part of an epsilon before settling on the letters αι
The scribe seems to have hesitated before writing the η, leading to a poorly executed letter.
The scribe seems to have written the α hesitantly or even to have begun another letter
The scribe seems to have hesitated before writing o, but the result is not to be treated as an omega.
The scribe seems to have begun η before writng the ending ει.
